statement: By the project's own admission, the provenance manifest proves 'what the resolver recorded at build time, not that a downstream agent behaved correctly' — a hash proves an output was recorded, not that a command ran — so its headline tamper-evidence/provenance feature is materially weaker than the SLSA/in-toto signing other tools provide and cannot be marketed as runtime or execution assurance.

evidence.quote: SLSA-compatible build provenance, cryptographic chain from source to artifact... Ed25519 signing + manifest verification.

falsifier: Ship the heavier supply-chain tier (Cosign signing + SLSA-style attestation) so provenance is cryptographically verifiable by a third party, not just an internally-recorded hash chain.
